headlines.headline: UK Defense Secretary Resigns, Warns Military Lacks Resources for Rising Threats
headlines.summary: John Healey resigned from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government, accusing it of failing to adequately fund the military amid growing global threats. The resignation raises concerns about NATO burden-sharing as the US considers cutting its European presence.

headlines.headline: Tech Executives from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google to Attend G7 Summit
headlines.summary: AI leaders including Sam Altman, Dario Amodei, and Demis Hassabis will join G7 leaders in France next week to discuss AI governance and online safety. The meeting comes as two of the companies prepare for landmark IPOs.

headlines.headline: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong Anti-G7 Protest
headlines.summary: Swiss authorities are boarding up businesses and deploying security ahead of a major anti-G7 protest expected this weekend. France hosts the summit next week in neighboring Évian-les-Bains.

headlines.headline: Oil Falls to Near Two-Month Lows as Trump Calls Off Iran Strikes
headlines.summary: Crude prices dropped over 4% after President Trump cancelled new strikes on Iran, reducing fears of escalation. The move signals potential de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict that has disrupted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Anti-Immigrant Unrest Sparks Violence in Belfast
headlines.summary: Riots and attacks targeting immigrants have spread in east Belfast, with Sudanese families among those targeted. The violence reflects growing tensions over immigration in Northern Ireland.

headlines.headline: US to slash fighter jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
headlines.summary: The US plans to significantly reduce aircraft and warships for NATO in Europe, including cutting F-16 and F-15E fighter jets from about 150 to 100, as Washington shifts focus. The move raises concerns among European allies about US commitment to continental defense.

perspectives.angle: US Plans Major Cuts to Fighter Jets and Warships for NATO Operations in Europe
perspectives.summary: The United States plans to significantly reduce aircraft and warships available for NATO operations in Europe, according to a New York Times report citing senior European officials. The move raises concerns about US commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: China cancels high-level diplomatic meetings with EU at short notice
headlines.summary: Beijing abruptly cancelled two diplomatic meetings with the European Union planned for June, Financial Times reports. The move signals growing tensions between China and Europe amid trade disputes and geopolitical friction.

perspectives.angle: China Cancels High-Level Diplomatic Meetings With EU in Beijing
perspectives.summary: China cancelled at short notice two diplomatic meetings planned with the European Union in Beijing this month, the Financial Times reported, raising questions about the state of EU-China relations.

headlines.headline: Iran conflict leaves world in dangerous limbo despite cease-fire
headlines.summary: Two months after a nominal cease-fire, Iran, Israel and the US remain locked in low-intensity conflict. Neither peace nor full war, the situation creates ongoing uncertainty for oil markets and regional stability.

headlines.headline: G7 summit to spotlight global economic imbalances as China exports surge
headlines.summary: France uses its G7 presidency to highlight mounting global trade tensions driven by China export surge, chronic US deficits, and weak European investment. The summit in Evian comes as world economy faces financial shock risks.

headlines.headline: German Army Chief Warns Russia Could Attack NATO by 2029
headlines.summary: German army chief Lt. Gen. Carsten Brecht warned that Russia will be prepared to attack NATO by 2029, citing "NATO-agreed intelligence." The warning adds to growing Western concerns about Russian military buildup near alliance borders.

perspectives.angle: German Army Chief Warns Russia Will Be Ready to Attack NATO by 2029
perspectives.summary: German army chief Christian Freuding stated Russia will be prepared to attack NATO by 2029, calling it "NATO-agreed intelligence" and the latest in a series of warnings about Russian threat and Europe lack of preparedness.

headlines.headline: South Asian and Gulf Leaders Intervened to Stop Trump Striking Iran
headlines.summary: A new report reveals South Asian and Gulf leaders raced to present Trump with a diplomatic alternative to military strikes on Iran, presenting him with a deal framework. The diplomatic intervention came as Trump had signaled imminent attacks.

headlines.headline: Israel Releases Hamas Co-Founder Hassan Yousef After Two Years Detention
headlines.summary: Israel has released Hamas co-founder Hassan Yousef after more than two years of detention. Yousef is a senior leader of Hamas in the West Bank, having co-founded the group in 1987 along with Sheikh Ahmed Yassin.

headlines.headline: China Confirms Arrest of US Citizen Suspected of Spying
headlines.summary: China has confirmed the arrest of a US citizen suspected of espionage activities. The arrest adds to growing tensions between Beijing and Washington amid ongoing disputes over trade, technology, and regional security.

headlines.headline: Ebola Outbreak Spreads in Congo as Cracked Coffin Traced to Super-Spreader Event
headlines.summary: A pastor's coffin that broke on the rocky road to his burial is suspected as one of the earliest super-spreader events in Congo's growing Ebola epidemic.

perspectives.angle: Two Babies Die of Ebola in Congo Orphanage as Outbreak Spreads
perspectives.summary: Two infants have died of Ebola in a Congo orphanage, highlighting the particular risk children face in the ongoing outbreak. WHO reports testing has been stalled in three labs due to supply shortages.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Exchange Overnight Drone Strikes, Targeting Energy and Rail Systems
headlines.summary: Russia and Ukraine carried out far-reaching drone strikes overnight, with Ukraine hitting a major oil processing region while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine's Drone Commander Plans to Cut Crimea Off From Russia
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's drone forces commander is mapping out a campaign to sever Crimea's connection to Russia using extensive battlefield data, targeting supply routes that have already left Crimean fuel stations dry.

headlines.headline: Iran war deal close as Strait of Hormuz tensions linger
headlines.summary: Hopes for peace grew after Trump said a deal could be signed this weekend, though Tehran said no final decision has been made on a pact. The strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a key sticking point in negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Trump says US "ended the war with Iran" but Tehran has yet to confirm deal
perspectives.summary: The US president said a peace deal could be signed within days and he may attend a signing ceremony. Iranian officials denied any final agreement had been reached, calling reports speculative.

headlines.headline: US and EU near deadline on $11.5 billion tariffs in decades-long aircraft dispute
headlines.summary: Washington and Brussels have yet to decide whether to continue suspending or reimpose tariffs on $11.5 billion of goods in a dispute over aircraft subsidies, with their truce expiring within days.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Chief Vows to Press on Despite Chinese Sanctions
headlines.summary: Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro vowed to continue his duties despite China sanctioning him for "erroneous remarks," calling out China's "wickedness" as tensions escalate over South China Sea disputes.

perspectives.angle: Philippines protests China sanctions against defense chief as unfriendly act
perspectives.summary: Manila condemned Beijing sanctions on Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro, calling them an "unfriendly act." China sanctioned Teodoro and his family for "erroneous remarks" about China, further escalating tensions between the two nations.

headlines.headline: UK Defense Minister Resigns Over Defense Funding
headlines.summary: UK Armed Forces Minister Al Carns resigned in protest over lack of defense funding, following Defense Secretary John Healey's earlier resignation. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a political crisis as two defense ministers quit within days.

headlines.headline: UAE Sends Military Planes to South Korea for Missile Defense Transport
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates dispatched military aircraft to South Korea to expedite transport of Korean-made Cheongung missile defense systems, highlighting deepening defense cooperation.

headlines.headline: ECB signals option to raise rates again in July after insurance hike
headlines.summary: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in nearly three years, with Bundesbank President Nagel saying all options remain open for July to combat Iran-war-driven energy inflation.

headlines.headline: UK lags European rivals in curbing influx of cheap parcels from China
headlines.summary: More cheap parcels are flowing into the UK from China than into rival European economies, undermining British retailers and raising concerns about the UKs ability to address the trade imbalance.

headlines.headline: World chess body suspends Russia after sports tribunal ruling
headlines.summary: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia following a Court of Arbitration for Sport ruling in favor of Ukraine, ending decades of Russian dominance in the game.

headlines.headline: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths in powerful Philippine earthquake
headlines.summary: Philippine officials say years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

headlines.headline: Fuel Stations in Crimea Run Dry After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
headlines.summary: Fuel stations on the Russian-held Crimean peninsula were out of petrol on Thursday as a Ukrainian campaign against supply lines escalates, highlighting the effectiveness of Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Fuel Supplies in Crimea, Creating Shortages on Russian-Held Peninsula
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces targeted fuel supplies to Crimea, causing a fuel crisis on the occupied peninsula in a new blow to Moscow's war narrative.

headlines.headline: British Defence Minister John Healey Resigns Over Spending Dispute
headlines.summary: UK Defence Minister John Healey quit on Thursday, accusing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of failing to commit the resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats. The resignation highlights tensions over military spending.

perspectives.angle: UK Defence Minister Resigns Over Military Spending Dispute
perspectives.summary: British Defence Minister John Healey quit over a months-long dispute with Prime Minister Keir Starmer over military spending, accusing him of failing to commit resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats.

headlines.headline: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong G7 Protest
headlines.summary: Swiss authorities are preparing for a massive anti-G7 protest expected this weekend in Geneva as France hosts the Group of Seven summit. Shopkeepers have boarded up businesses ahead of the demonstration.

perspectives.angle: Geneva Prepares for 50,000-Strong G7 Protest
perspectives.summary: Swiss authorities are bolstering security in Geneva ahead of a massive anti-G7 demonstration as France hosts the summit, with shopkeepers already boarding up businesses.

headlines.headline: Japan-South Korea Summit Aims to Deepen Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung held a summit on May 19, discussing enhanced cooperation in an increasingly volatile geopolitical environment.

headlines.headline: France, UK, Germany Envoys Meet Russian Foreign Ministry Officials
headlines.summary: Ambassadors from France, the UK, and Germany arrived at the Russian Foreign Ministry for meetings with senior diplomats, amid heightened tensions over the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Germany and France Scrap FCAS Fighter Jet Project
headlines.summary: In a major blow to European defense cooperation, Germany and France have abandoned their ambitious FCAS fighter jet project after years of planning and billions in investment.

headlines.headline: South Korea ex-President Yoon sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone plot
headlines.summary: A Seoul court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for ordering military drones to infiltrate North Korea, intended to create a pretext for his failed December 2024 martial law declaration.

perspectives.angle: South Korea's Former President Yoon Sentenced to 30 Years Over North Korea Drone Incident
perspectives.summary: A South Korean court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for ordering military drones to fly into North Korea, in a case involving the Pyongyang drone incident.

headlines.headline: Trump says "great" Iran settlement will reopen Strait of Hormuz within days
headlines.summary: President Trump announced a "great settlement" with Iran could be signed within days, which would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end hostilities. Oil prices extended losses on the news.

perspectives.angle: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz, Attacks US Bases in Kuwait and Bahrain
perspectives.summary: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels and launched retaliatory strikes on US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan following American attacks. The escalation marks day 104 of the conflict as tit-for-tat strikes continue.

headlines.headline: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups meet in France to push for two-state solution
headlines.summary: Israeli and Palestinian civil society representatives will meet in France on Friday, urging the international community not to abandon the two-state solution as Paris works to keep the issue alive amid the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, says deal "approved" after key points agreed
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump called off further strikes on Iran and announced that key points of a peace deal have been agreed, with a formal agreement expected within days.

headlines.headline: Ukraine War Has Lasted Longer Than World War I
headlines.summary: The war in Ukraine has now exceeded the duration of World War I, with parallels emerging between the grinding nature of both conflicts and the way new technologies have reshaped warfare. The milestone underscores the protracted nature of the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine War Duration Exceeds World War I
perspectives.summary: The conflict in Ukraine has now lasted longer than World War I, marking a grim milestone as fighting continues with no clear end in sight. The war has entered its fourth year with ongoing casualties and infrastructure destruction.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Haggle Over Frozen Funds as Interim Deal Nears
headlines.summary: Iran and the United States are discussing how to release frozen Iranian funds as both sides work toward an interim deal to end hostilities. Despite ongoing military strikes from both sides, diplomatic efforts have intensified.

headlines.headline: China Sanctions Philippine Defense Secretary Over South China Sea Remarks
headlines.summary: China's foreign ministry announced sanctions against Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro and his close relatives, accusing him of making "erroneous remarks" that undermined China's legitimate interests and bilateral ties.

headlines.headline: Wartime Apology Author Yohei Kono Dies at 89
headlines.summary: Yohei Kono, the Japanese politician who authored the historic 1993 Kono Statement apologizing to comfort women and served as a bridge between Japan and China, died at 89. He was reportedly planning a trip to ease bilateral tensions.

headlines.headline: Haaretz Analysis: Water Crisis May Bring Iran to Its Knees
headlines.summary: A Haaretz analysis examines how Iran's severe water crisis, exacerbated by mismanagement and drought, poses an existential threat to the regime beyond military conflicts.

headlines.headline: At least 15 dead in Pakistan-administered Kashmir protests over reserved legislative seats
headlines.summary: Deadly clashes between protesters and security forces have killed at least 15 people in Pakistan-administered Kashmir. The violence erupted after activists demanded abolition of reserved assembly seats for refugees from Indian-administered Kashmir.

perspectives.angle: At Least 15 Dead in Deadly Clashes in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir
perspectives.summary: Violent clashes in Pakistan-administered Kashmir have left at least 15 people dead, marking a significant escalation in regional tensions. The unrest adds to ongoing security challenges in South Asia.

headlines.headline: Russia expanding military buildup near NATO border, investigation reveals
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery and intelligence reports show Russia is expanding military infrastructure along its borders with Finland, Norway, and the Baltic states. Nordic intelligence chiefs warn the buildup is preparation for potential conflict with NATO.

perspectives.angle: Russia Expands Military Infrastructure Near NATO Border
perspectives.summary: An investigation reveals Russia has expanded military infrastructure near its border with NATO members and is preparing for potential conflict across all domains—land, air, sea, space, and cyber.

headlines.headline: European Countries Ramp Up Defense Spending Amid Rising Russia Threat
headlines.summary: Twenty-two of NATO's 29 European members have now increased defense budgets above the 2% GDP threshold, responding to growing security concerns from Russia's war in Ukraine and uncertainty over US commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Germany Army Chief Warns: "We Must Be Ready to Fight" Against Russia
headlines.summary: German Army chief Christian Freuding issued a stark warning that Germany must be prepared for armed conflict, citing the growing Russian threat. The statement comes as European nations scramble to strengthen military capabilities.

headlines.headline: China Bars Philippine Defense Chief From Entry Over South China Sea Dispute
headlines.summary: China imposed an entry ban on Philippine Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro amid ongoing tensions in the South China Sea. Manila has urged Beijing to remove a "movable platform" at a disputed shoal as confrontations continue.

headlines.headline: OPEC Cuts 2026 Oil Demand Forecast Again as Iran War Disrupts Markets
headlines.summary: OPEC lowered its world oil demand growth forecast for 2026 to 970,000 barrels per day—the second straight downward revision—as the Iran conflict reshapes global energy markets.

perspectives.angle: OPEC Projects Oil Demand Growth to Outpace Supply Through 2027
perspectives.summary: OPEC maintains its view of a relatively tight oil market through next year, with demand growth expected to outpace supply despite lowering its 2026 demand growth forecast.

headlines.headline: Slovenia Lifts Netanyahu Entry Ban Under New Government
headlines.summary: Slovenia new conservative government abolished an entry ban on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, reversing measures taken by the previous administration over Gaza.

headlines.headline: North Korea Voices Full Support for Russia on National Day
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un sent a congratulatory message to Putin expressing full support for Russia domestic and foreign policies, reinforcing the deepening Pyongyang-Moscow alliance.

headlines.headline: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47 after long illness
headlines.summary: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the eldest daughter of King Maha Vajiralongkorn, has died at 47 after nearly four years in a coma. The princess was known for her legal work and had been in poor health since December 2022.

perspectives.angle: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47 after years in coma
perspectives.summary: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, the eldest daughter of King Maha Vajiralongkorn and a lawyer known for her advocacy on women rights in prisons, has died at age 47 after nearly four years in a coma.

headlines.headline: US plans to deport Iranians to Central African Republic
headlines.summary: The Trump administration plans to deport Iranian and other migrants to the Central African Republic, a chronically unstable country facing violence and poverty, according to lawyers and officials briefed on the matter.

headlines.headline: Toronto police officer killed in shooting linked to US Consulate attack
headlines.summary: A Toronto police officer was shot and killed by a suspect linked to an investigation into a shooting attack on the US Consulate in March. Const. Marc Pinizzotto was part of a team executing a search warrant.

headlines.headline: Tornadoes touch down outside Chicago as severe storms pummel Midwest
headlines.summary: Damaging storms swept through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands. New tornadoes were reported outside Chicago as the severe weather system moved through.

headlines.headline: Iranian State Media Says No MoU Text Agreed With US
headlines.summary: Iran has not approved any text for an initial memorandum of understanding with the United States, Iran semi-official Fars news agency reported, citing a source close to the negotiating team.

headlines.headline: Dominican Republic Pushes Tax Hikes to Offset Iran War Oil Surge
headlines.summary: The Dominican government plans to increase or implement new taxes to generate some $800 million in additional revenue annually to offset a surge in oil prices blamed on the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Screwworm Parasite Threatens Texas Cattle Industry
headlines.summary: A screwworm parasite outbreak is threatening cattle in Texas, sparking a political blame game as agricultural officials work to contain the pest that can kill livestock within days.

headlines.headline: Trump Says US Has Ended the War With Iran
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump announced that the United States has "ended the war" with Iran, though Tehran has yet to confirm any formal agreement, as diplomatic negotiations continue.

headlines.headline: France 24 Live: Iran Claims Hormuz Closure as US Strikes Continue
headlines.summary: France 24 provides ongoing coverage of the Middle East conflict as Iran announces the closure of the Strait of Hormuz following US attacks, with the US denying the claim.

headlines.headline: CNA Analysis: How US-Iran Strikes Show Diplomacy Has Changed
headlines.summary: Channel News Asia commentary examines how the exchange of strikes between the US and Iran reflects a fundamental shift in how modern diplomacy operates during active conflicts.

headlines.headline: Lebanons Christians Flee Tyre, Fear Israel Will Block Return
headlines.summary: Christian residents of southern Lebanons port city of Tyre are fleeing despite the Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire, fearing Israeli military actions may prevent their return.

headlines.headline: Senate Defense Bill Rejects IVF Coverage Expansion
headlines.summary: The Senate Armed Services Committee rejected a provision in the annual defense policy bill that would have expanded IVF coverage for service members.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Vows to Expel Chinese Coast Guard Patrols From Its Waters
headlines.summary: Taiwan condemned Chinese coast guard patrols near its eastern shores as violations of sovereignty and vowed to expel any challengers, raising cross-strait tensions as Beijing asserts jurisdiction claims.

headlines.headline: Northern Ireland Police Fire Plastic Bullets at Race Riots
headlines.summary: Police in Northern Ireland fired plastic bullets to contain race riots, with reinforcements called in from Great Britain as violence escalated amid rising tensions.

headlines.headline: Man Pleads Guilty to Killing Top Minnesota Democrat and Her Husband
headlines.summary: A man pleaded guilty to the political assassination of the top Democrat in the Minnesota House and her husband while posing as a police officer, in a case that shocked state politics.

headlines.headline: Analysis: Is the Iran Ceasefire Over? What Latest US Attacks Tell Us
headlines.summary: A wave of US strikes represents the gravest test yet of the fragile truce with Iran. Analysts examine what happened, what officials are saying, and whether the deal can survive.

headlines.headline: Global markets rally on hopes for Iran peace deal
headlines.summary: MSCI global equities index rallied while the dollar and bond yields fell on renewed hopes for Middle East peace after Trump canceled planned strikes against Iran.

perspectives.angle: European Bonds Rally as Trump Touts Progress on Iran Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: European bonds surged while oil prices slid on renewed prospects for a Middle East peace deal, with Trump indicating an agreement could be reached soon.

headlines.headline: Iran fights to preserve Lebanon as leverage in US settlement talks
headlines.summary: Iran is waging a calculated campaign to keep Lebanon as its last bastion of Mediterranean influence, tying the country to a grand bargain with Washington as it seeks to end Hezbollah's war with Israel on its own terms.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall as Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, raising peace hopes
headlines.summary: Oil prices settled lower after President Trump canceled plans to strike Iran, raising expectations for a deal to end more than three months of war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Japanese business sentiment sours as Middle East war hits economy
headlines.summary: Japanese business sentiment turned negative for the first time in four quarters, a government survey showed, reflecting growing economic pain from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump to nominate Jay Clayton as Director of National Intelligence
headlines.summary: President Trump plans to nominate Jay Clayton, the US Attorney for the Southern District of New York, as Director of National Intelligence, following Congress rejection of a FISA extension tied to spy chief appointments.

headlines.headline: IDF Poised for Hezbollah Offensive in Nabatieh
headlines.summary: Israeli military officials say armor and troops have been covertly moved into position for an attack on Hezbollah stronghold in southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh.

perspectives.angle: Israel Prepares Offensive Against Hezbollah in Nabatieh
perspectives.summary: The Israeli military is poised to launch an offensive against Hezbollah in the southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh, one of the key Hezbollah strongholds. IDF officials say preparations are complete.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Cuba State Oil Company
headlines.summary: The State Department announced sanctions against Cuba's state-owned oil and gas company Unión Cuba-Petróleo, expanding pressure on the island nation.

headlines.headline: British Arms Dealer Convicted of Selling Fighter Jets to Warlords
headlines.summary: A Londoner and Greek co-defendant were convicted of supplying planes, helicopters, guns, bombs, grenades and missiles to conflict zones around the world.

headlines.headline: World Bank cuts global growth forecast to 2.5% citing Iran war
headlines.summary: The World Bank cut its 2026 global growth forecast to 2.5% due to the Middle East conflict, warning growth could slow to 1.3% if energy supply disruptions prove more severe.

perspectives.angle: European Central Bank Raises Interest Rate as First Major Bank to Respond to Iran War
perspectives.summary: The ECB became the first major central bank to raise interest rates in response to the Iran conflict, as energy price shocks from Middle East tensions push inflation concerns across Europe.

perspectives.angle: ECB Raises Interest Rates First Time Since 2023 Amid War-Led Inflation
perspectives.summary: The European Central Bank raised interest rates by 25 basis points, lifting the deposit rate to 2.25%, aiming to curb inflation before energy cost surges from the Iran war spread across the eurozone economy.

headlines.headline: US House rejects FISA extension, spy powers set to expire
headlines.summary: The Republican-controlled House rejected a short-term extension of foreign surveillance powers sought by President Trump, deepening a political standoff over his appointment of an inexperienced loyalist as top spy chief.

perspectives.angle: FISA surveillance powers set to expire after Congress fails to act
perspectives.summary: A key US surveillance tool that allows intelligence collection abroad appears certain to expire after Congress failed to extend the powers, deepening a political standoff over the spy chief appointment.

headlines.headline: Trump calls off Iran strikes, says peace deal imminent
headlines.summary: President Trump canceled planned military strikes on Iran at the last minute, saying negotiations with Tehran were heading toward a possible deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: Trump Cancels Iran Strikes, Says Peace Deal Could Be Signed This Weekend
perspectives.summary: President Trump called off planned military strikes against Iran, announcing that a peace agreement has been "approved" and could be signed in Europe this weekend. The announcement follows days of escalating conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran.

headlines.headline: US-Canada bridge opening delayed after Trump objections
headlines.summary: The opening of a new $4.7 billion bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor has been delayed after President Trump earlier this year threatened to block the crossing.

headlines.headline: China joins Macron-led video call in rare economic talks ahead of G7 summit
headlines.summary: Chinese Vice Premier Zhang Guoqing will attend a video conference hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron on global economic imbalances, days before G7 countries meet in France to discuss how to deal with waves of low-priced Chinese exports entering their markets.

headlines.headline: Iran and Ukraine loom over G7 as France accommodates Trump
headlines.summary: Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine are set to dominate next week's Group of Seven summit, as host France crafts an agenda aimed at projecting unity and avoiding confrontation with U.S. President Donald Trump.

perspectives.angle: Iran and Ukraine Loom Over G7 as France Accommodates Trump
perspectives.summary: The G7 summit in France faces twin crises over Iran and Ukraine as host President Macron seeks to balance competing priorities while accommodating Trump approach to foreign policy.

headlines.headline: China Expands Gulf Drone Market Share as Iran War Creates Opening
headlines.summary: The ongoing US-Iran conflict has provided China an opportunity to increase its defense sales to Gulf states, offering cheaper and faster drone deliveries with fewer restrictions than Western suppliers.

headlines.headline: Russia Central Bank Chief Nabiullina Disappears from Public View Amid Resignation Rumors
headlines.summary: Elvira Nabiullina has not been seen in public for over a week, missing the St. Petersburg forum. Reports link her possible resignation to concerns about border closures and martial law.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support of Beirut Government
headlines.summary: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman ordered the resumption of Lebanese imports, offering relief to businesses hit by years of Hezbollah-Israel conflict and showing support for Lebanon's embattled government.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support for Beirut Government
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia has lifted a roughly five-year ban on imports from Lebanon in a show of support for Beiruts embattled government, offering relief to businesses battered by years of conflict between Hezbollah and Israel.

headlines.headline: Slovenia lifts entry ban on Netanyahu as new government shifts Israel policy
headlines.summary: Slovenia's new conservative government abolished an entry ban on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and two of his ministers, rolling back several Gaza-related measures adopted by the previous administration.

headlines.headline: FIDE suspends Russia over chess federation activities in occupied Ukraine
headlines.summary: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia after it failed to relinquish control of chess bodies in five Ukrainian regions captured by Russian forces. Ukraine called it a "historic victory" for the sport.

headlines.headline: El Niño officially begins, scientists warn of extreme weather threats
headlines.summary: US scientists confirmed El Niño has officially started, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures globally. The climate event could bring severe heat, floods, and droughts worldwide.

headlines.headline: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession in el-Obeid
headlines.summary: Rights groups accused RSF paramilitary of killing civilians in a drone strike on a funeral procession in el-Obeid, a city on Sudan's civil war front line.

headlines.headline: UN Chief Calls for Return to US-Iran Ceasefire
headlines.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres expressed deep concern over continued escalation in the Middle East and called for an immediate return to ceasefire negotiations between the US and Iran.

headlines.headline: Graham Backs Seizure of Iranian Kharg Island as Game Changer
headlines.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham expressed support for President Trump threat to seize Iran Kharg Island, calling it the ultimate game changer in the conflict. The island is critical to Iran oil exports.

headlines.headline: US Confirms Third Strike on Indian-Crewed Tanker Off Oman
headlines.summary: A US jet fired missiles into the engine room of the tanker Jalveer off Oman, marking the third strike on Indian-crewed vessels this week as the Iran conflict disrupts Gulf shipping.

perspectives.angle: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Commercial Vessel Off Oman
perspectives.summary: Three Indian sailors died after a US strike hit a commercial vessel off the coast of Oman. New Delhi has demanded an end to US attacks on ships after the incident raised tensions with a key partner.

perspectives.angle: Indian-crewed tanker hit off Oman in suspected third US strike
perspectives.summary: An asphalt tanker with 20 Indian crew members was hit in a suspected US strike off Oman, with all crew reported safe.

perspectives.angle: India Protests to US After Strike Kills Three Indian Sailors
perspectives.summary: India has formally protested to the United States after a strike killed three Indian sailors near Oman, with 21 of 24 crew members rescued from the vessel Settebello.

headlines.headline: US Senate Panel Approves $750 Million Ukraine Aid Extension
headlines.summary: The Senate Armed Services Committee voted to extend US security assistance for Ukraine with $750 million in authorized funding, pushing back against the Trump administration aid pullback.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Opposition Leader Says Xi Meeting Avoided Reunification Talk
headlines.summary: Taiwan opposition figure Eric Cheng revealed his meeting with Xi Jinping avoided reunification discussions, as Taiwan awaits approval of a $14 billion US arms package.

headlines.headline: Trump Threatens to Seize Iranian Oil Export Hub Kharg Island
headlines.summary: President Trump warned the U.S. would strike Iran "very hard" and expressed intent to take control of Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export terminal, as the U.S.-Iran conflict reignites following a shaky ceasefire.

perspectives.angle: Trump vows US will seize Iranian oil hub Kharg Island
perspectives.summary: President Trump said the United States would hit Iran 'very hard tonight' and threatened to eventually take control of Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export terminal, as the conflict between Washington and Tehran reignited.

headlines.headline: U.S., Iran Discuss Frozen Funds as Interim Deal Efforts Intensify
headlines.summary: Despite ongoing strikes, the U.S. and Iran are negotiating how to release frozen Iranian funds as part of efforts to reach an interim deal to end hostilities, with both sides seeking a path to de-escalation.

headlines.headline: Turkey Mediates Between US and Iran as Conflict Reignites
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan advised the US and Iran to stop the reignited conflict during diplomatic discussions. The meeting also addressed Black Sea security and EU relations as regional tensions escalate.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Rupiah Hits Record Low as Fiscal Pressures Mount
headlines.summary: Indonesia rupiah has become Asia worst-performing currency this year as economic pressures mount. The World Bank forecasts GDP growth slowing to 5% in 2026 amid fiscal strains.

perspectives.angle: Indonesia's rupiah slide raises deeper fiscal concerns despite emergency measures
perspectives.summary: Indonesia's emergency interest rate hike and efforts to support the rupiah have calmed markets temporarily, but economists warn investor confidence won't fully return without addressing deeper fiscal issues.

headlines.headline: Oil prices steady as markets assess Hormuz closure fallout
headlines.summary: Oil prices remained nearly flat on Thursday as investors weighed potential supply disruptions from the Middle East conflict after Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz following US military strikes.

perspectives.angle: Strait of Hormuz Closure Sends Oil Prices Soaring, Impacts India
perspectives.summary: Global oil prices jumped sharply after Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with significant implications for India as a major energy importer.

headlines.headline: Pakistan budget hits middle class to meet IMF targets amid Iran war pressure
headlines.summary: Pakistan's government proposed a budget hitting the middle class and registered businesses to raise revenue and cut spending while shielding the poorest, as the Iran war and IMF requirements squeeze the economy.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Approves Record Defense Budget Increase for 2026
headlines.summary: Ukraine is increasing defense and security spending by $34.6 billion, bringing total 2026 expenditures to a record $97.6 billion. The funds will support weapons purchases, military pay, and critical infrastructure protection.

headlines.headline: India Demands End to US Ship Attacks After Three Sailors Killed
headlines.summary: Three Indian sailors died in a US military strike on a tanker off Oman as part of Washington's blockade of Iran-linked shipping. India summoned a US diplomat to demand an end to the attacks, calling for dialogue and diplomacy.

headlines.headline: Hegseth's Blocked Promotions Draw Fire From Former Military Leaders
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's targeted blocks on promotions of officers in the Navy and Air Force have intensified scrutiny from former military leaders, who call the moves unprecedented and a corrosive abuse of power. A disproportionate number of the blocked promotions were women or minorities.

headlines.headline: Bloomberg Opinion: ECB Jumping at Old Inflation Shadows
headlines.summary: The European Central Bank raised rates to 2.25%, its first hike since 2023, but critics argue the move may be responding to past inflation patterns rather than current economic realities. The decision comes as the Iran war threatens new energy price shocks.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Deploys Ground-Based Drones to Shift Frontline Duties from Soldiers
headlines.summary: Ukraine is increasingly relying on robotic ground drones to punch above its weight on the battlefield, automating logistics and reconnaissance tasks previously handled by soldiers. The technology represents a significant evolution in modern warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok shrine bombing
headlines.summary: A Thai military court convicted two ethnic Uyghur men from Xinjiang for the 2015 Erawan Shrine bombing that killed 20 people and wounded over 100, delivering death sentences after a nine-year trial.

perspectives.angle: Thai Court Sentences Two Uyghurs to Death for 2015 Bangkok Shrine Bombing
perspectives.summary: A Thai court handed death sentences to two ethnic Uyghur men for the 2015 Erawan Shrine bombing that killed 20 people. The attack was one of Thailand deadliest acts of violence.

headlines.headline: Italy PM calls for single EU voice in Russia peace talks
headlines.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni urged European allies to appoint a single representative for talks with Russia to end the Ukraine war, questioning whether smaller groups can represent the entire bloc.

headlines.headline: Thai woman faces murder charges in Myanmar for killing of US diplomat
headlines.summary: A Thai woman appeared in a Myanmar court on murder charges in the killing of an American diplomat found dead in Yangon, in a case that could strain diplomatic relations in Southeast Asia.

perspectives.angle: US Diplomat Found Dead in Myanmar, Thai Woman in Custody
perspectives.summary: A US State Department official was found dead in Yangon with a Thai woman in custody. The department confirmed the fatality but declined to provide additional details about the circumstances.

headlines.headline: Iran warns ceasefire "practically meaningless" after US strikes
headlines.summary: Iran warned that the shaky three-month ceasefire was now "practically meaningless" after US strikes, signaling potential further escalation.

headlines.headline: Russian envoy says Mideast tensions threaten global economy
headlines.summary: Kremlin spokesman called for restraint from all parties, warning that escalating Middle East tensions pose risks to the entire global economy.

headlines.headline: Three injured in overnight US strikes on Iran
headlines.summary: US strikes overnight Thursday wounded at least three people at sites in Tehran province, Iranian media reported.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Exchange Fire for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
headlines.summary: The US and Iran traded military strikes for a second consecutive day, with Tehran launching retaliatory attacks on bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan while Washington continued its bombing campaign. President Trump vowed further strikes unless Tehran agrees to immediate peace talks.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Trade Air Attacks for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
perspectives.summary: The United States and Iran exchanged air attacks for a second straight day, with President Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The renewed fighting has cast fresh doubt over prospects for ending the conflict.

headlines.headline: US Overtakes Saudi Arabia and Russia as World's Top Oil Exporter
headlines.summary: The United States has become the world's largest oil exporter, ending decades of Saudi and Russian dominance. The shift tightens American control over global energy markets as Washington's conflict with Iran reshapes trade flows.

headlines.headline: Global Strategic Oil Reserves Depleting Rapidly Amid Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves worldwide are running out fast more than 100 days into the third Gulf war, as nations draw down stockpiles to offset supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure.

headlines.headline: Thailand Reconsiders CPTPP Membership Amid Global Trade Shifts
headlines.summary: Thailand is reassessing joining the CPTPP alongside an EU trade deal, as aggressive US tariffs and rising protectionism shake the global trading system.

headlines.headline: Turkey Suspends BYD Tax Break Over $1 Billion Investment Plan
headlines.summary: The Turkish government has halted import tax exemptions for Chinese EV maker BYD, warning of potential clawbacks if the company fails to meet its $1 billion investment commitment.

headlines.headline: US Bases in Middle East Face Second Round of Iranian Retaliatory Attacks
headlines.summary: US military bases across the Middle East are experiencing a second wave of Iranian retaliatory strikes following earlier American attacks. The escalation threatens to further destabilize the already fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Heads to Canary Islands to Highlight Migrant Crisis
headlines.summary: Pope Leo traveled to the Canary Islands to meet migrants who have braved dangerous Atlantic crossings, urging more humane treatment of immigrants as the Mediterranean route faces record arrivals.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands to highlight perilous migrant journeys
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo traveled to Spain's Canary Islands, a major migration hotspot, warning that history will condemn leaders who ignore migrant deaths and calling for humane treatment of those fleeing war and poverty.

headlines.headline: Gulf Countries Say Iran Aggression Closes Door to Dialogue
headlines.summary: Gulf Arab states have condemned Irans recent attacks, stating that Tehrans aggression has effectively ended any prospect for diplomatic engagement in the near term.

headlines.headline: US Military Confirms No Warships Struck in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: The US military has confirmed that none of its naval vessels were hit in the Strait of Hormuz, amid Iranian claims of successful strikes on American assets.

headlines.headline: Trump to Meet Defense Firms Over Weapons Shortage Concerns
headlines.summary: The White House is convening defense industry leaders to discuss ramping up production amid growing concerns over missile supply and depleted stockpiles from ongoing conflicts.

headlines.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026 Kicks Off Amid Geopolitical Tensions
headlines.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup begins with Iran war and visa restrictions casting a shadow over football's biggest tournament. Politics looms large as the US co-host prepares to perform under global scrutiny.

perspectives.angle: World Cup 2026 Opens Amid North American Tensions
perspectives.summary: The FIFA World Cup kicks off in Mexico with 48 nations competing, but the tournament is overshadowed by strained relations between the US and its neighbors. Mexico and Canada have faced significant recent diplomatic tensions with the Trump administration.

headlines.headline: G7 Summit Opens with Tight Security on Swiss-French Border
headlines.summary: French and Swiss authorities imposed pandemic-like border restrictions as G7 leaders including President Trump gathered for the summit. Security measures are intense amid concerns about potential violent protests.

headlines.headline: UK Venture Plans Europe's Largest Direct Air Carbon Capture Plant
headlines.summary: A new UK company called UnionDAC aims to become a leader in removing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere, planning Europe's largest direct air capture facility as climate technology investment accelerates.

headlines.headline: US Strikes Hit Iranian Drinking Water Facility Near Hormuz
headlines.summary: US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz reportedly hit an Iranian drinking water facility, raising concerns about civilian infrastructure targeting.

perspectives.angle: Satellite images suggest Israeli air base hit in Iranian missile strikes
perspectives.summary: Analysis of satellite imagery indicates possible damage to a hangar at Ramat David Air Force Base following Iran's recent missile attacks, despite Israeli claims of minimal impact.

headlines.headline: IRGC Claims Targeting US Fighter Jet Base and Military Facilities in Jordan
headlines.summary: Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps says it targeted US military facilities including a fighter jet base in Jordan in retaliatory strikes.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Over 100 Million Barrels of Oil Passed Through Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump claims significant oil volumes continue through the strategic Strait of Hormuz despite Iranian threats to close the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: US Becomes Worlds Top Oil Exporter, Upending Decades-Old Global Energy Order
headlines.summary: The United States has become the worlds largest oil exporter, overtaking Saudi Arabia and Russia in a historic shift that tightens American companies grip on energy markets as Washingtons war with Iran reshapes global energy trade.

headlines.headline: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Campaign Continues
headlines.summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Conflict Escalates
perspectives.summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah, with the Iran-backed group claiming fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

headlines.headline: Iran and US exchange fire as hostilities escalate across Gulf
headlines.summary: Iran retaliated against US strikes with attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, while the US military announced it completed additional strikes on multiple Iranian targets. Bahrain activated missile warning sirens and Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace.

headlines.headline: Dollar wobbles as Middle East strikes unsettle markets
headlines.summary: The US dollar fluctuated on Thursday as fresh US military strikes in the Middle East undermined sentiment, while a surge in May consumer inflation to a three-year high kept investors uneasy about the Federal Reserve outlook.

headlines.headline: Trump praises Armenia PM election win despite Russian pressure
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump congratulated Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on his Civil Contract party decisive victory in weekend elections, noting the win came despite significant Russian pressure on the country.

headlines.headline: EU fiscal watchdog criticises loosening budget rules for energy transition
headlines.summary: The European Fiscal Board warned that allowing governments to spend defence fiscal flexibility on clean energy transition undermines EU fiscal discipline, amid debates over how to fund both priorities.

headlines.headline: Indian shares set to open lower on Middle East escalation and US inflation
headlines.summary: Indian equities are likely to open lower as fresh escalation between the US and Iran and a sharp jump in US inflation trigger risk-off sentiment across global markets.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV blesses Gaudis Sagrada Familia on centenary of architects death
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV celebrated Mass at Barcelonas iconic Sagrada Familia basilica exactly 100 years after architect Antoni Gaudis death, blessing the towering architectural masterpiece.

headlines.headline: Five EU countries call for united response to Trump drug price threats
headlines.summary: Five EU member states are pushing for a coordinated European response to US pressure on pharmaceutical pricing, warning against bilateral deals that could undermine the bloc.

headlines.headline: UK-EU summit at risk as youth visa talks hit fresh obstacle
headlines.summary: A planned UK-EU summit faces delays as negotiations over youth mobility visas stall, with both sides struggling to find common ground on migration terms.

headlines.headline: India calls in air force to secure exam papers after scandal fuels protests
headlines.summary: The Indian Air Force has been tasked with transporting and securing examination papers after cheating scandals sparked nationwide protests over exam integrity.

headlines.headline: Police Fire Water Cannons at Belfast Protesters as Unrest Flares After Stabbing
headlines.summary: Northern Ireland saw renewed violence as police used water cannons against protesters in Belfast following a stabbing incident. A Sudanese man appeared in court charged with attempted murder.

headlines.headline: Trump White House UFC Fight Requires $60M and 7 Federal Agencies
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump's planned UFC fight on the White House South Lawn requires a monumental effort from more than seven federal agencies and an estimated $60 million in costs.

headlines.headline: Japan Restarts Reactor at World's Largest Nuclear Plant Amid Energy Push
headlines.summary: A reactor at the world's largest nuclear power plant in Japan has resumed operations as the country pushes to maximize nuclear power generation to cope with growing electricity needs and climate goals.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Sovereign Wealth Fund Tests Investor Appetite With Debut Dollar Bond
headlines.summary: Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time of market volatility and global uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Chinese AI Police Tech Measures Vital Signs and "Core Motivation" at Beijing Expo
headlines.summary: An international police equipment expo in Beijing showcased surveillance cameras that measure vital signs and even "core motivation" of suspects, reflecting China's advancing AI-powered security capabilities.

headlines.headline: House Lawmakers Back 5-7% Military Pay Raise for 2027, No Increase for DoD Civilians
headlines.summary: The House Armed Services Committee approved a 5% to 7% pay raise for service members in 2027, while Defense Department civilian employees would see no pay increase under the spending bill.

headlines.headline: India's Urban Boom Turns Cities Into Heat Traps as Climate Change Intensifies
headlines.summary: India's rapid urbanization is making cities significantly hotter, with the urban heat island effect combining with climate change to create dangerous living conditions for millions.

headlines.headline: Bahrain sounds missile alert sirens as Iran vows retaliation for US strikes
headlines.summary: Bahrain activated its missile alert system as Iran announced it would retaliate after the US launched new airstrikes. The tiny Gulf kingdom hosts the US Fifth Fleet and is within range of Iranian missiles.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu to seek re-election despite Trump doubts
headlines.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu will run for re-election this year, his party announced, after US President Donald Trump said he was not sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.

headlines.headline: Analysis suggests precision US strikes hit Iranian water facility
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery and video analysis indicate US strikes may have hit a water treatment facility in Iran. It remains unclear whether the facility was deliberately targeted or struck inadvertently.

headlines.headline: US human rights officials meet North Korea detainee families in Seoul
headlines.summary: Senior US human rights officials visiting South Korea met with families of South Koreans detained or abducted by North Korea, amid ongoing concerns about Pyongyang's human rights record and nuclear program.

headlines.headline: UN to send investigators to Lebanon to assess potential war law violations
headlines.summary: The UN human rights office will deploy a team of investigators to Lebanon next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties during the ongoing conflict. The move comes amid continued Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets.

headlines.headline: Chinese propagandists stoking dissent over Trump tariffs and data centres, OpenAI says
headlines.summary: OpenAI reports that Chinese-speaking users were asked to use ChatGPT to create slogans and cartoons criticizing the US president's trade policies, highlighting ongoing influence operations.

headlines.headline: Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese firms tests Xi's US visit
headlines.summary: Washington's latest move highlights intense competition between the US and China, testing the fragile "stability" reached at the leadership summit in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Massive new US strikes rock multiple targets inside Iran
headlines.summary: Iranian state media networks confirm huge explosions across Bandar Abbas, Sirik and the nearby city of Minab in the southern part of the country amid a new wave of US attacks.

headlines.headline: Explosions and airstrikes reported across southern Iran amid new US attacks
headlines.summary: Explosions and airstrikes were reported across southern Iran early Thursday amid a new wave of US attacks, according to local reports.

headlines.headline: Ukraine police chief: Russia recruits young women to kill Ukrainian servicemen
headlines.summary: Ukraines police chief accused Russia of recruiting teenage Ukrainian girls to assassinate military personnel, following the arrest of a 17-year-old suspected of murdering a serviceman on instructions from a Russian operative.

headlines.headline: China-North Korea claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinpings visit to North Korea elevated Kim Jong Uns global stature and pulled Pyongyang closer into Beijings orbit, though analysts note significant limits remain in the bilateral relationship.

perspectives.angle: Xi Jinping wraps up rare North Korea visit with deeper cooperation pledge
perspectives.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping completed his first visit to North Korea in seven years, establishing a deeper understanding and clearer path for development of ties between the two countries.

headlines.headline: Trump sticks with Pulte for intel job as risk grows of lapse in spy powers
headlines.summary: A lapse in US foreign intelligence-gathering powers has grown more likely as President Trump continues to back his nominee for the intelligence post amid congressional gridlock.

headlines.headline: UN Nuclear Watchdog Demands Iran Report Enriched Uranium Stocks
headlines.summary: The IAEA's 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining enriched uranium stocks and allow inspectors to verify them, a move that could complicate Washington's ongoing talks with Tehran.

headlines.headline: Erdogan warns Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon now threaten Turkey
headlines.summary: Turkish President Erdogan stated that Israel's military operations in Syria and Lebanon have escalated to the point where they threaten Turkey itself, calling Israel's aggression a threat to the whole world.

perspectives.angle: Erdogan warns Israel attacks on Syria and Lebanon threaten Turkiye directly
perspectives.summary: Turkish President Erdogan said Israeli operations in Syria and Lebanon have escalated to the point where they pose a direct threat to Turkey, calling the aggression a danger to the entire region.

headlines.headline: Ukraine partners with Taurus missile maker for deep strike systems
headlines.summary: Ukrainian defense company Ukrainian Armor and European firm MBDA initiated a strategic partnership to develop deep strike capabilities, enhancing Ukraine's indigenous defense production.

headlines.headline: Countries vie to become energy safe haven as Hormuz crisis deepens
headlines.summary: Multiple nations are pitching the Trump administration on energy alternatives as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, seeking to position themselves as secure energy suppliers.

headlines.headline: Ireland sets online safety, security priorities for EU presidency
headlines.summary: Ireland's EU presidency will focus on improving online safety for children and boosting Europe's economic competitiveness and security amid growing geopolitical challenges.

headlines.headline: Violent Unrest Erupts in Belfast Over Knife Attack Arrest
headlines.summary: Police in Northern Ireland used water cannons against protesters as violent unrest flared in Belfast following the arrest of a Sudanese man charged with attempted murder in a stabbing attack, with officials calling for calm.

perspectives.angle: Elon Musk accused of inflaming tensions in Belfast after knife attack sparks violence
perspectives.summary: The tech billionaire shared lists of protest locations on social media and blamed "uncontrolled immigration" for the knife attack in Belfast, which triggered anti-immigrant violence. The victim's family appealed for calm amid ongoing disorder.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda Hospitalised, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
headlines.summary: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalised for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, the central bank announced, raising uncertainty about Japans monetary policy direction during a critical period.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan Governor Hospitalized, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
perspectives.summary: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalized for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, where the central bank was expected to raise interest rates to 1.0% amid inflation concerns.

headlines.headline: Israel Kills 16 in Lebanon as UN Announces International Law Probe
headlines.summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 16 people as UN investigators announced they will arrive next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties in the escalating conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Says He Loves Inflation as Prices Hit 3-Year High
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump dismissed concerns about surging consumer prices, saying I love the inflation and claiming the U.S. is taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil, as inflation reached its highest level in three years.

headlines.headline: NATO Forward Land Forces Stand Up New Multinational Battlegroup in Finland
headlines.summary: NATO has stood up its Forward Land Forces Finland during a ceremony at the Swedish Army base, establishing the alliances newest multinational battlegroup as part of its enhanced deterrence posture in Northern Europe.

headlines.headline: Trump Threatens New Military Action Against Iran After Helicopter Downing
headlines.summary: President Trump warned of additional strikes on Iran, accusing Tehran of "playing us for suckers" after a US helicopter was downed near the Strait of Hormuz. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

headlines.headline: China Re-centers North Korea Ties as Nuclear Silence Reshapes Regional Balance
headlines.summary: As Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang, China is reasserting its influence with North Korea at a time when Kim Jong Un has remained silent on nuclear activities. The diplomatic move signals Beijing effort to counter US pressure in the region.

headlines.headline: US Navy Establishes Naval Support Activity in Western Australia Under AUKUS Pact
headlines.summary: As part of the AUKUS security pact, NSA Stirling will provide support for personnel assigned to Submarine Rotational Forces, strengthening Western military presence in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: ICEYE and Rheinmetall Form German Joint Venture for Space-Based ISR
headlines.summary: Finnish SAR satellite company ICEYE and German defense giant Rheinmetall have launched a joint venture for space-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities.

headlines.headline: NASA Unveils Artemis III Crew to Test Technology for Future Moon Landing
headlines.summary: NASA announced four astronauts for the Artemis III mission: Randy Bresnik, Frank Rubio, Andre Douglas and ESA astronaut Luca Parmitano. They will orbit Earth while practicing docking with lunar landers targeted for 2027.

perspectives.angle: NASA names Artemis III crew for next step toward Moon landing
perspectives.summary: The space agency announced the four astronauts who will crew the Artemis III mission in 2027, marking the next major milestone in America's return to lunar exploration.

headlines.headline: Two Merchant Vessels Damaged in Ukrainian Black Sea Corridor Attack
headlines.summary: A Panamanian-flagged and a Barbadian-flagged vessel were damaged while transiting Ukraines Black Sea navigation corridor, highlighting ongoing risks to commercial shipping despite the corridors establishment.

headlines.headline: India Accelerates Military Revamp to Counter China and Pakistan
headlines.summary: India is moving forward with Integrated Theatre Commands that would unite the army, navy and air force in its biggest military shake-up in decades, reflecting growing concerns about simultaneous two-front threats.

headlines.headline: IAEA Board Passes Resolution Demanding Iran Report Uranium Stocks
headlines.summary: The UN nuclear watchdogs 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining uranium stocks, as concerns grow about Irans nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: US Tungsten Scrap Exports to Japan Soar on Chinese Curbs
headlines.summary: Japan-bound US shipments of recycled tungsten have surged dramatically as China restricts exports of the critical industrial metal, highlighting supply chain vulnerabilities in strategic materials.

headlines.headline: Israeli Lawmakers Advance Bill to Exempt Ultra-Orthodox from Military Service
headlines.summary: Israeli MPs voted to advance legislation that would allow most ultra-Orthodox Jewish men to avoid conscription, a controversial move amid ongoing war and domestic political tensions.

headlines.headline: US Forces Disable Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman for Blockade Violation
headlines.summary: US Central Command announced that its forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker transiting toward Iran for alleged violation of the blockade, marking the latest enforcement action in the Gulf.

perspectives.angle: US Military Disables Oil Tanker Headed to Iran in Gulf of Oman
perspectives.summary: US forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman as the unladen vessel headed toward Iran, escalating enforcement of the US naval blockade amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

headlines.headline: UN Chief Warns of Return to Full-Scale War in Middle East
headlines.summary: Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned of the risk of returning to full war in the Middle East after Iran and the United States traded strikes. The UN called for immediate de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations.

headlines.headline: EU and South Korea Sign Digital Trade Pact
headlines.summary: The European Union and South Korea signed a digital trade agreement to facilitate digital transactions and strengthen economic ties. The pact comes as both sides seek to diversify trade relationships amid global uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Foreign Outflows Surge from Asian Equities on Middle East Risks
headlines.summary: Asian equity markets are experiencing significant foreign outflows in June as investors retreat from risk amid escalating Middle East hostilities and a pullback in AI-linked technology stocks. The trend highlights regional vulnerability to geopolitical shocks.

headlines.headline: All Aboard Military Helicopter Killed in Pakistan Kashmir Crash
headlines.summary: A Pakistani military helicopter crashed near Muzaffarabad in Pakistani Kashmir, killing all personnel on board. The military did not specify the number of casualties in the incident.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan military helicopter crashes in Kashmir killing at least 22
perspectives.summary: An army Mi-17 helicopter crashed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir due to technical fault, officials said. The crash sparked clashes that killed at least 15 more in the region.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Launches Deadly Airstrikes on Afghanistan, Killing at Least 13 Including Children
perspectives.summary: Pakistani airstrikes targeted three Afghan provinces killing at least 13 people including 11 children, the Taliban government said, marking a major escalation in border tensions.

headlines.headline: Russian and Ukrainian Drones Stray Into NATO Countries as Aerial War Spills Over
headlines.summary: Drones launched by both Russia and Ukraine are veering off course, entering airspace of NATO members Lithuania and Romania, raising concerns about alliance escalation as the conflict's aerial dimension expands beyond combat zones.

perspectives.angle: Drones from Ukraine war straying into NATO territory, raising tensions
perspectives.summary: Russian and Ukrainian drones are veering off course and entering airspace of NATO countries like Lithuania and Romania, creating security concerns for nations not directly involved in the conflict.

headlines.headline: OPEC Output at Lowest Since 2000 as US Blockade Squeezes Iran
headlines.summary: OPEC oil production in May fell to its lowest level in over two decades as the US naval blockade cut Iran's exports and Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments from other Gulf producers.

perspectives.angle: Global Oil Stockpiles Headed Toward Lowest Levels Since 2003 Amid Iran Conflict
perspectives.summary: Oil inventories in major economies are approaching multi-decade lows as the Iran war draws down reserves at a record pace, the US Energy Information Administration warned.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Fires Rockets Toward China in Defensive Drill Using US Launchers
headlines.summary: Taiwan's military conducted a live-fire exercise sending rockets in China's direction from US-supplied HIMARS mobile launching systems, demonstrating defensive capabilities amid heightened cross-strait tensions.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan test-fires US-supplied HIMARS toward China in first public display
perspectives.summary: Taiwan staged its first use of the US-supplied HIMARS missile system in the Taiwan Strait as part of invasion-defense drills, a significant military demonstration amid ongoing tensions with Beijing.

headlines.headline: Trump and Iran Trade New Threats After Military Strikes
headlines.summary: President Trump warned Iran will "pay the price" for delaying negotiations while Tehran vowed retaliation, as both sides exchanged threats following US strikes and Iran's downing of a US helicopter.

headlines.headline: Iran Ceasefire Crumbles as Trump Says Tehran Will Pay Price for Failed Talks
headlines.summary: A US-Iran ceasefire appears to have collapsed after Trump warned Iran will face consequences for not reaching a deal, with fresh US strikes and Iranian retaliation reported.

headlines.headline: Iran War Threatens Millions With Crisis-Level Hunger, World Food Programme Warns
headlines.summary: Ripple effects from the war in Iran are threatening millions more vulnerable people with crisis levels of hunger, the World Food Programme chief said.

headlines.headline: Qatari negotiators travel to Tehran to narrow gaps in US-Iran talks
headlines.summary: Qatari mediators are working to bridge remaining differences between Washington and Tehran as ceasefire talks continue. The diplomatic effort comes amid fragile truce conditions.

headlines.headline: US and allies condemn Iran for lethal plotting in Europe and North America
headlines.summary: The US, Britain, and other allies issued a joint condemnation of Iran's IRGC for plotting assassinations and intimidation campaigns on Western soil, highlighting Tehran's global reach.

headlines.headline: Cargo vessel exchanges fire with armed craft off Yemen coast
headlines.summary: A commercial ship reported engaging with an armed vessel 88 nautical miles southwest of Yemen, raising concerns about renewed Houthi or pirate activity in the Red Sea region.

headlines.headline: US-Taiwan drone relationship shifts as Taipei develops own exports
headlines.summary: While the US traditionally sells weapons to Taiwan, the island's growing drone industry is now developing systems for potential export, marking a reversal in defense trade dynamics.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping Visits North Korea, Deepens Ties with Kim Jong Un
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping wrapped up his first visit to North Korea in seven years, agreeing to expand cooperation with Kim Jong Un. Both leaders claimed major wins but analysts note limits remain, particularly on Pyongyang nuclear program.

headlines.headline: Argentine Peso Drops Most in Eight Weeks After Midterm Selloff
headlines.summary: Argentina peso is facing renewed decline as investors grow more risk-averse following midterm election results. The drop highlights ongoing economic challenges facing the country recovery efforts.

headlines.headline: Masked Men Burn Families Out of Homes in Belfast Anti-Immigrant Riots
headlines.summary: A wave of anti-immigrant violence swept Belfast after a Sudanese man was charged with attempted murder for a knife attack. Masked men torched homes and vehicles across Northern Ireland.

perspectives.angle: Belfast Anti-Immigrant Protests Erupt After Knife Attack Injures Four
perspectives.summary: Northern Ireland leaders called for calm after anti-immigrant violence broke out in Belfast following a stabbing that injured four people. Homes and vehicles were set alight as protests spread across the city.

headlines.headline: Erdogan declares Turkiye security begins from Aleppo, Damascus and Beirut
headlines.summary: The Turkish president stated that his countrys security perimeter extends into Syria and Lebanon, as Israel continues operations against Hezbollah and other targets in those countries.

headlines.headline: Ukraine warns Russian attacks on Odesa ports threaten grain export collapse
headlines.summary: Russian strikes on Black Sea ports have damaged export terminals, threatening significant reduction in agricultural shipments. Ukraine's largest farmers' union warns of dire consequences for global food supplies.

headlines.headline: EU proposes 21st sanctions package against Russia targeting banks and crypto
headlines.summary: The European Union unveiled its latest sanctions package, heavily targeting Russian banks, crypto networks, drone production, and oil traders. EU chief diplomat Kaja Kallas announced the measures.

headlines.headline: Amnesty accuses Israel of ethnic cleansing of West Bank Palestinians
headlines.summary: Amnesty International has accused Israel of carrying out a campaign of "ethnic cleansing" against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, saying measures are designed to accelerate annexation of Palestinian territory.

headlines.headline: Pakistan claims killing 26 Afghan fighters; Taliban says civilians died
headlines.summary: Pakistan targeted TTP fighters across the Afghan border, with the Taliban reporting 11 children among those killed in air strikes. The strike marks an escalation in cross-border tensions.

headlines.headline: Taiwan test fires US rocket system toward Chinese mainland for first time
headlines.summary: Taiwan fired HIMARS rockets toward China in its first such test, a significant escalation of military demonstrations as cross-strait tensions continue to rise.

headlines.headline: Greece approves faster migrant returns and plans return hubs
headlines.summary: The Greek parliament approved legislation to accelerate the return of migrants who do not qualify for asylum and aims to establish return processing hubs, part of broader EU efforts to manage migration flows.

headlines.headline: Philippines to pursue maritime border talks with Japan despite China opposition
headlines.summary: Manila plans to delimit sea boundaries with Tokyo, a move Beijing opposes as both nations face separate maritime disputes with China in the East China Sea and South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Pentagon reveals preferred munitions for one-way attack drones
headlines.summary: The US Defense Department has published a list of preferred payloads for loitering munitions, with Northrop Grumman and several startups competing to supply low-cost warheads compatible with various drone platforms.

headlines.headline: US approves $2 billion counter-drone sale to Kuwait after Iranian strikes
headlines.summary: The State Department cleared a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms to Kuwait, following recent Iranian attacks on the Gulf state amid escalating regional conflict.

headlines.headline: South Korea warned on fragmented air defenses amid drone swarm threat
headlines.summary: Military and industry experts urged Seoul to urgently build an integrated air and missile defense system to counter evolving drone swarm threats from North Korea.

perspectives.angle: South Korea Warned on Fragmented Air Defenses Against Drone Swarms
perspectives.summary: Military and academic experts warn South Korea must urgently build an integrated air and missile defense system to counter emerging drone swarm threats.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan to raise rates to 1.0% as inflation pressures mount
headlines.summary: The BOJ is set to raise its key interest rate this month and again in Q4, reaching 1.25% by year-end, as it becomes more wary of inflation risks from energy shocks and a weak yen.

headlines.headline: China prepares $295 billion AI infrastructure buildout to challenge US dominance
headlines.summary: Beijing is planning to spend around 2 trillion yuan over five years on data center infrastructure across the country, accelerating its competition with Washington in the AI race.

headlines.headline: Taliban Crack Down on Anti-Hijab Protest in Western Afghanistan
headlines.summary: Afghan security forces dispersed a women's rights protest in Herat after Taliban morality police detained women for violating dress codes, marking another escalation in the regime's restrictions on women's freedoms.

headlines.headline: Dollar Holds Steady as Markets Brace for US-Iran Tensions and Inflation Data
headlines.summary: The US dollar remained stable against major currencies as investors monitored escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran while awaiting key inflation data that could influence Federal Reserve rate decisions.
